summary: DHH responds to a dismissive characterization of Ruby as just another passing fad scripting language. He points out how the critic cleverly used language to avoid engaging with Ruby on its merits, calling it both a 'latest obsession' and 'yet another interpreted scripting language.' DHH argues this rhetorical framing serves as a defense mechanism to avoid the intellectual effort of actually evaluating the language.
key_points: Critics dismissed Ruby simultaneously as a passing phase and as redundant among scripting languages
DHH identifies this double dismissal as an intellectual defense mechanism to avoid genuine engagement
Early advocacy for Ruby faced reflexive resistance from the established programming community
money_quotes: På den måde så har man da sikret sig ikke at skulle besvære sit intellekt med den byrde rent faktisk at kigge nærmere på sproget.
key_insight: Dismissing new technologies with rhetorical shortcuts is an intellectual defense mechanism that avoids the burden of genuine evaluation.
